sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.71 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Consider a free group$F$of finite rank and an arbitrary group$G$. Define the$G$-closure\/${ cl}_G(T)$of any subset$T F$as the intersection of the kernels of all those homomorphisms$:F G$of$F$into$G$that vanish on$T$: \${ cl}_G(T) = \,\{ { Ker}\, \, \, :F G;\;\, T { Ker}\, \}$. \ Groups$G$and$H$are called geometrically equivalent\/ if for every free group$F$and every subset$T F$the$G$- and$H$-closu...
